2604.19464.title: LePREC: Reasoning as Classification over Structured Factors for Assessing Relevance of Legal Issues
2604.19464.paper_summary: More than half of the global population struggles to meet their civil justice needs due to limited legal resources. While Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated impressive reasoning capabilities, significant challenges remain even at the foundational step of legal issue identification. To investigate LLMs' capabilities in this task, we constructed a dataset from 769 real-world Malaysian Contract Act court cases, using GPT-4o to extract facts and generate candidate legal issues, annotated by senior legal experts, which reveals a critical limitation: while LLMs generate diverse issue candidates, their precision remains inadequate (GPT-4o achieves only 62%). To address this gap, we propose LePREC (Legal Professional-inspired Reasoning Elicitation and Classification), a neuro-symbolic framework combining neural generation with structured statistical reasoning. LePREC consists of: (1) a neuro component leverages LLMs to transform legal descriptions into question-answer pairs representing diverse analytical factors, and (2) a symbolic component applies sparse linear models over these discrete features, learning explicit algebraic weights that identify the most informative reasoning factors. Unlike end-to-end neural approaches, LePREC achieves interpretability through transparent feature weighting while maintaining data efficiency through correlation-based statistical classification. Experiments show a 30-40% improvement over advanced LLM baselines, including GPT-4o and Claude, confirming that correlation-based factor-issue analysis offers a more data-efficient solution for relevance decisions.
2604.19464.paper_summary_zh: 全球人口中有超過一半因法律資源有限而難以滿足其民事司法需求。雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）展示了令人印象深刻的推理能力，但在法律問題識別的基礎步驟上仍然存在重大挑戰。為了調查LLMs在這項任務中的能力，我們從769個真實的馬來西亞合同法法庭案件中構建了一個數據集，使用GPT-4o提取事實並生成候選法律問題，這些問題由資深法律專家註釋，顯示出一個關鍵的限制：雖然LLMs生成了多樣的問題候選，但其精確度仍然不足（GPT-4o的精確度僅為62%）。為了解決這一差距，我們提出了LePREC（法律專業啟發的推理引導與分類），這是一個結合神經生成與結構化統計推理的神經符號框架。LePREC包括：（1）一個神經組件利用LLMs將法律描述轉換為代表多樣分析因素的問答對，以及（2）一個符號組件對這些離散特徵應用稀疏線性模型，學習明確的代數權重，以識別最具信息量的推理因素。與端到端的神經方法不同，LePREC通過透明的特徵加權實現可解釋性，同時通過基於相關性的統計分類保持數據效率。實驗顯示，相較於先進的LLM基準，包括GPT-4o和Claude，LePREC在性能上提高了30-40%，確認基於相關的因素-問題分析提供了一種更具數據效率的相關決策解決方案。
2604.19464.author: Fanyu Wang et.al.
2604.19464.authors: Fanyu Wang, Xiaoxi Kang, Paul Burgess, Aashish Srivastava, Chetan Arora, Adnan Trakic, Lay-Ki Soon, Md Khalid Hossain, Lizhen Qu
